rulings.Case Title: VERNON FARQUHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 5, DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED, DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF STATED KAISER GYPSUM COMPOUND WAS SANDED BY DEFENDANT IN PLAINTIFF'S PRESENCE, PLAINTIFF DID NOT ASSERT THAT DEFENDANT WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE COMPOUND OR THAT THE COMPOUND CONTAINED ASBESTOS. THE AYE DECLARATION DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BECAUSE IT FAILS TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A FINDING IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: VERNON FARQUHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 5, DEFENDANT TODD SHIPYARDS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff's discovery responses are not factually devoid. Defendant may be held liable for the negligent acts of its employees. Here, the record reflects that defendants' employees regularly swept up asbestos materials at the direction of defendant. This conduct exposed plaintiff to asbestos. Plaintiff's declaration also establishes a triable issue of fact and is an excellent illustration of how to provide a foundation for testimony that materials contain asbestos. The objections to the declaration are overruled. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GARY SHORT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 7, DEFENDANT SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by showing a lack of evidence to link Mr. Short to defendant's employees. The Declaration of Nance of October 3, 2007 does not create a triable issue of fact. It fails to provide a basis for a trier of fact to conclude that the debris and dust generated from sweeping the floors contained asbestos and/or that defendant is responsible for that asbestos. Mr. Cohen's re-entrainment theory has not be accepted by the Court's of California and the facts here do not change that result. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE BERG et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 8, DEFENDANT ANDERSON, ROWE, AND BUCKLEY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF BY DEMONSTRATING THAT ROBERT CANTLEY'S DEPOSITION IDENTIFIED IN SPECIAL INTERROGATORY ANSWERS DID NOT SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. FURTHER, CANTLEY'S DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE AND CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE BERG et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 8, DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues of fact based on the Mingram Declaration if plaintiff's file an executed original of the Declaration. Defendant's objection to the declaration is overruled. The Court cannot conclude that the failure to disclose Mingram in response to the special interrogatories was willfully done. Defendant's other criticisms of the Mingram declaration go to its ultimate persuasiveness, not to the question as to whether there is a triable issue of fact. The Court has not relied on the Ay Declaration in finding a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 12, DEFENDANT EATON ELECTRICAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS ELLIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 12, DEFENDANT SQUARE D COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on the special interrogatory responses of plaintiff. Plaintiff's opposition creates triable issues of fact based upon plaintiff's declaration and deposition, defendant's response to interrogatory 31 and the Cohen declaration. Motion for summary adjudication as to punitive damages is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on interrogatory responses 23-26 and plaintiff presented no evidence of malice. As to the other causes of action, the motion is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof on the cause of action for false representation because there is no special interrogatory addressing that subject. As to the causes of action for negligence and strict liability, the issue is causation and triable issues of fact have been established by plaintiff. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 14, DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTORS SALES U.S.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RUFUS RENARD BROWN VS. BRENT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY INC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 15, DEFENDANTS BRENT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY INC. AND MIKE SCRIBNER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Labor Code § 515(e) requires a showing that plaintiff be primarily engaged, meaning more than 50% of the time, in exempt work where one customarily and regularly utilizes discretion and independent judgment at a monthly salary twice the minimum wage for full-time employment. The only element that defendant has been able to conclusively meet is the wage requirement. Even if it were determined that defendant shifted the burden of proof, plaintiff has presented facts in opposition that create triable issues of fact as to whether plaintiff should be classified as an exempt or non exempt employee. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT S BULLEN VS. LEV YUROVSKY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 2, 2007, LINE 19, DEFENDANTS LEV YUROVSKY, TATYANA YUROVOSKY, ANDREY YUROVSKY, AND TAMARA YUROBSKY'S MOTION TO STRIKE is denied. The decision in Birkner v. Lam, 2007 WL 2730260, provides the framework for the Court's decision. Defendants' motion is based on the letter of August 7, 2006 and the owner move-in notice. Under Birkner, those documents reflect protected petitioning activity. However, plaintiff's complaint is based on more than these two documents as reflected in paragraphs 10 and 11. Further, at most only the 1st, 3rd and 8th causes of action could be said to rely upon protected conduct; however, a reading of the complaint demonstrates such conduct is only a part of the defendant's conduct about which plaintiff complains. As a consequence, the Court does not find that plaintiff's complaint is based upon protected activity. Assuming, however, that it is, plaintiff has presented prima facie facts that plaintiff is likely to prevail on the causes of action alleged. Plaintiff's declaration describes supporting a claim of retaliation, harassment and habitability. The fact that defendants did not follow through on the owner move-in creates a presumption in the tenant's favor under section 37.9(d) of the Rent Ordinance. Although the litigation privilege may protect some of defendants' conduct, it is not a bar to the vast majority of facts upon which plaintiff relies. =(302/PJM/PB)
